title: Naming conventions for macos Lex swift

content.text:
Given the naming conventions we used for both Zig and Swift. Discuss with me to apply it to @macos/Lex.swift 

Don't provide example code, don't write any code.

content.thinking: Let me start by looking at the macos/Lex.swift file to understand its current structure before we discuss naming conventions.

result.content:
1: import Carbon
2: import SwiftUI
3: 
4: @main
5: struct LexApp: App {
6:     @NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor(AppDelegate.self)
7:     private var appDelegate
8: 
9:     var body: some Scene {
10:         MenuBarExtra {
11:             LexMenu(
12:                 appDelegate: appDelegate,
13:                 appState: appDelegate.appState
14:             )
15:         } label: {
16:             LexMenuBarLabel(appState: appDelegate.appState)
17:         }
18:         .menuBarExtraStyle(.menu)
19:     }
20: }
21: 
22: struct LexMenuBarLabel: View {
23:     @ObservedObject var appState: AppState
24: 
25:     var body: some View {
26:         Label("Lex", systemImage: appState.mode == .telex ? "pencil" : "pencil.slash")
27:         .labelStyle(.iconOnly)
28:         .accessibilityLabel("Lex")
29:     }
30: }
31: 
32: // Namespace: LEX!
33: private let toggleInputModeHotKeyId = EventHotKeyID(signature: OSType(0x4C455821), id: 1)
34: 
35: final class AppDelegate: NSObject, NSApplicationDelegate {
36:     @MainActor
37:     let appState = AppState()
38: 
39:     private var engineState: UnsafeMutableRawPointer?
40:     private var signalSources: [DispatchSourceSignal] = []
41:     private var toggleInputModeHotKeyRef: EventHotKeyRef?
42:     private var toggleInputModeSound: NSSound?
43: 
44:     func applicationDidFinishLaunching(_ notification: Notification) {
45:         print("Activated.")
46:         installSignalHandler(SIGINT)
47:         installSignalHandler(SIGTERM)
48:         initializeEngineState()
49:         registerToggleInputModeHotKey()
50:         // Start event tap.
51:     }
52:     
53:     func applicationWillTerminate(_ notification: Notification) {
54:         print("Shutting down...")
55:         // Stop event tap.
56:         unregisterToggleInputModeHotKey()
57:         destroyEngineState()
58:         signalSources.removeAll()
59:     }
60: 
61:     private func initializeEngineState() {
62:         precondition(engineState == nil)
63:         let byteCount = Int(lex_state_size)
64:         let alignment = Int(lex_state_alignment)
65:         let state = UnsafeMutableRawPointer.allocate(
66:             byteCount: byteCount,
67:             alignment: alignment
68:         )
69:         lex_state_init(state)
70:         engineState = state
71:     }
72: 
73:     private func destroyEngineState() {
74:         guard let state = engineState else {
75:             return
76:         }
77:         state.deallocate()
78:         engineState = nil
79:     }
80: 
81:     private func installSignalHandler(_ signalNumber: Int32) {
82:         signal(signalNumber, SIG_IGN)
83:         let source = DispatchSource.makeSignalSource(signal: signalNumber, queue: .main)
84:         source.setEventHandler {
85:             NSApp.terminate(nil)
86:         }
87:         source.resume()
88:         signalSources.append(source)
89:     }
90: 
91:     private func registerToggleInputModeHotKey() {
92:         let selfPointer = Unmanaged.passUnretained(self).toOpaque()
93:         var eventType = EventTypeSpec(eventClass: OSType(kEventClassKeyboard), eventKind: UInt32(kEventHotKeyPressed))
94:         InstallEventHandler(
95:             GetApplicationEventTarget(),
96:             { _, event, userData -> OSStatus in
97:                 guard let userData else {
98:                     return OSStatus(eventNotHandledErr)
99:                 }
100:                 var hotKeyId = EventHotKeyID()
101:                 GetEventParameter(
102:                     event!,
103:                     EventParamName(kEventParamDirectObject),
104:                     EventParamType(typeEventHotKeyID),
105:                     nil,
106:                     MemoryLayout<EventHotKeyID>.size,
107:                     nil,
108:                     &hotKeyId
109:                 )
110:                 if hotKeyId.signature == toggleInputModeHotKeyId.signature &&
111:                         hotKeyId.id == toggleInputModeHotKeyId.id {
112:                     let appDelegate = Unmanaged<AppDelegate>
113:                         .fromOpaque(userData)
114:                         .takeUnretainedValue()
115:                     Task { @MainActor in
116:                             appDelegate.toggleInputMode()
117:                     }
118:                 }
119:                 return noErr
120:             },
121:             1,
122:             &eventType,
123:             selfPointer,
124:             nil
125:         )
126:         let id = toggleInputModeHotKeyId
127:         RegisterEventHotKey(
128:             UInt32(kVK_Space),
129:             UInt32(controlKey | optionKey),
130:             id,
131:             GetApplicationEventTarget(),
132:             0,
133:             &toggleInputModeHotKeyRef
134:         )
135:     }
136: 
137:     private func unregisterToggleInputModeHotKey() {
138:         guard let toggleInputModeHotKeyRef else {
139:             return
140:         }
141:         UnregisterEventHotKey(toggleInputModeHotKeyRef)
142:         self.toggleInputModeHotKeyRef = nil
143:     }
144: 
145:     @MainActor
146:     private func toggleInputMode() {
147:         appState.mode = appState.mode == .telex ? .literal : .telex
148:         // TODO: this flow must change when finish integrating with engine.
149:         toggleInputModeSound?.stop()
150:         let soundName: NSSound.Name = appState.mode == .telex ? "Pop" : "Tink"
151:         toggleInputModeSound = NSSound(named: soundName)
152:         toggleInputModeSound?.play()
153:     }
154: }
155: 
156: enum InputMode {
157:     case literal, telex
158: }
159: 
160: @MainActor
161: final class AppState: ObservableObject {
162:     @Published var mode: InputMode = .telex
163: }
164: 
165: struct LexMenu: View {
166:     let appDelegate: AppDelegate
167:     @ObservedObject var appState: AppState
168: 
169:     var body: some View {
170:         Button("Lock inputs") {}
171:         Divider()
172:         Button("Quit") {
173:             NSApp.terminate(nil)
174:         }
175:     }
176: }
177:

content.thinking: I'm checking the Lex.swift file against the naming conventions to make sure functions, variables, constants, and enum values follow snake_case while types use PascalCase.

content.text:
Audit against the conventions (Function/Variable/Constant/Enum value: `snake_case`; Type: `PascalCase`):

**Types (already compliant — PascalCase)**
- `LexApp`, `LexMenuBarLabel`, `AppDelegate`, `AppState`, `InputMode`, `LexMenu` ✓

**Enum values — need rename**
- `InputMode.literal`, `InputMode.telex` are already single lowercase words, so they're trivially `snake_case`. No change needed, but worth confirming you want to keep them as-is (e.g., not `.input_literal` / `.input_telex`).

**Functions — violate convention (currently camelCase)**
- `installSignalHandler` → `install_signal_handler`
- `initializeEngineState` → `initialize_engine_state`
- `destroyEngineState` → `destroy_engine_state`
- `registerToggleInputModeHotKey` → `register_toggle_input_mode_hot_key`
- `unregisterToggleInputModeHotKey` → `unregister_toggle_input_mode_hot_key`
- `toggleInputMode` → `toggle_input_mode`

Caveat: AppKit lifecycle methods `applicationDidFinishLaunching(_:)` and `applicationWillTerminate(_:)` are part of `NSApplicationDelegate` — those signatures are fixed by Apple's API. We cannot rename them. Should the convention explicitly carve out an exception for framework-imposed selectors/overrides?

**Variables / properties — violate convention (currently camelCase)**
- `appDelegate` → `app_delegate` (declared in `LexApp` and `LexMenu`)
- `appState` → `app_state` (in `LexApp`, `LexMenuBarLabel`, `LexMenu`, `AppDelegate`)
- `engineState` → `engine_state`
- `signalSources` → `signal_sources`
- `toggleInputModeHotKeyRef` → `toggle_input_mode_hot_key_ref`
- `toggleInputModeSound` → `toggle_input_mode_sound`
- Local: `selfPointer` → `self_pointer`, `eventType` → `event_type`, `hotKeyId` → `hot_key_id`, `signalNumber` → `signal_number`, `byteCount` → `byte_count`, `soundName` → `sound_name`

**Constants — violate convention**
- `toggleInputModeHotKeyId` → `toggle_input_mode_hot_key_id` (file-level `let`)

**Open questions for you**
1. SwiftUI property wrappers (`@ObservedObject var appState`, `@NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor var appDelegate`) work fine with `snake_case` — just confirming we apply the rename even though it looks unusual in SwiftUI idioms.
2. Function parameter labels (e.g., `notification`, `signalNumber`) — do you want these renamed too, or only the internal parameter name?
3. The hot-key ID acronym "Id": stay `id` (→ `hot_key_id`) rather than `i_d`? I'd keep `id` whole.
4. Carbon/AppKit API names (`InstallEventHandler`, `RegisterEventHotKey`, `kVK_Space`, etc.) are external — untouched. Confirm.
5. The protocol-required delegate methods are the only forced exception I see. Anything else you'd exempt (e.g., `body`, `label` from SwiftUI)? Note `body` is already lowercase/single word so it's fine.

Want me to proceed with all of the above once you've answered 1–5?
Yes, apply the change for our managed code, about the Platform / Frameword managed code, keep them as is.

content.thinking:
I'm identifying which method names and APIs are managed by frameworks and should stay unchanged—like the AppDelegate lifecycle methods, SwiftUI's body property, and the MenuBarExtra closure labels—while being careful not to rename anything that's part of the framework's public interface.

For our own code, I need to apply the snake_case convention consistently. The stored properties like `appDelegate` and `appState` should become `app_delegate` and `app_state`, which will automatically update the memberwise initializer labels in `LexMenu` and `LexMenuBarLabel`. I'm working through each identifier to determine whether it's framework-imposed or something we control. gle_input_mode`, `sound_name`, and keeping `InputMode`, `LexMenu`, and `AppState` as-is. I also need to update the call sites for `LexMenu` and `LexMenuBarLabel` since their initializers will use the renamed parameter names.

Now I'm writing out the full file with all these changes applied.

content.text:
Renames applied to [macos/Lex.swift](file://<redacted>/lex/macos/Lex.swift).

Kept as-is (framework-managed):
- `applicationDidFinishLaunching(_:)`, `applicationWillTerminate(_:)` — `NSApplicationDelegate` selectors
- `body`, `label:` closure label — SwiftUI requirements
- Property wrappers, Carbon/AppKit symbols, framework argument labels (`byteCount:`, `alignment:`, `signal:`, `queue:`, `eventClass:`, `eventKind:`, `systemImage:`, `named:`, etc.)

Renamed (ours):
- Properties: `app_delegate`, `app_state`, `engine_state`, `signal_sources`, `toggle_input_mode_hot_key_ref`, `toggle_input_mode_sound`
- Constant: `toggle_input_mode_hot_key_id`
- Functions: `install_signal_handler`, `initialize_engine_state`, `destroy_engine_state`, `register_toggle_input_mode_hot_key`, `unregister_toggle_input_mode_hot_key`, `toggle_input_mode`
- Locals/params: `signal_number`, `byte_count`, `self_pointer`, `event_type`, `hot_key_id`, `sound_name`, inner `app_delegate`
- Init labels for `LexMenu` and `LexMenuBarLabel` updated to match the renamed stored properties.

Not verified by build — no Swift toolchain invoked here. Recommend running the macOS build to confirm.
